seoTitle: Stephenston vs Stewart Range — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Stephenston vs Stewart Range: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stephenston and Stewart Range.
heading: Stephenston vs Stewart Range — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stephenston, South Australia and Stewart Range, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stephenston has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Stewart Range (31/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stephenston or Stewart Range safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stephenston has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Stewart Range scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stephenston scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Stewart Range scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stephenston or Stewart Range?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stephenston has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
